summary: Overall UK risk remains Elevated. External security shocks remain the key system-shaping factor, with the Bank of England explicitly noting that conflict-driven increases in global energy and commodity prices will raise near-term CPI inflation and can weaken economic activity. Domestically, the most tangible near-term disruption risk is concentrated in NHS operational pressure during ongoing resident-doctor industrial action rather than acute governance instability; cyber risk remains elevated due to widely deployed edge products facing urgent patching requirements.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: The Bank of England continues to frame conflict-driven energy/commodity price shocks as a near-term inflation and growth risk; latest CPI remains above target and energy remains a key pass-through channel for UK household costs.
drivers: BoE held Bank Rate at 3.75% (19 March 2026) and warned conflict-driven rises in global energy/commodity prices will raise near-term CPI inflation and can weaken activity
ONS CPI inflation was 3.0% y/y in February 2026 (release date 25 March 2026)
Brent-linked energy pricing remains a key sensitivity for UK fuel and utilities

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: The UK terrorism baseline remains SUBSTANTIAL, and ongoing external conflicts continue to pose spillover risks (energy, shipping, and broader security uncertainty) relevant to UK resilience.
drivers: MI5 states the UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely)
BoE notes conflict in the Middle East has driven global energy and other commodity prices higher, underscoring continued geopolitical spillover channels
Ongoing Russia–Ukraine and Middle East conflict environment sustains elevated background security risk

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: No new evidence in reviewed official sources indicates an acute UK governance crisis; current pressure appears focused on managing economic and service-delivery impacts rather than institutional instability.
drivers: No new reviewed official signals of immediate UK constitutional or governance instability
Policy attention remains focused on managing economic resilience and public-service delivery pressures
External security environment continues to create policy-management load

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: No signal in reviewed sources of an imminent UK-wide severe weather emergency; current hazard picture appears localised and should be monitored through official flood/weather services.
drivers: No reviewed official evidence today of UK-wide severe hazard escalation
Flood risk and severe weather conditions can change quickly; rely on official warning services for time-critical updates
Seasonal spring transition can still produce local flooding and travel disruption

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: Disruption risk remains elevated due to ongoing resident-doctor strike action in England, with expected knock-on impacts for elective activity and operational resilience even where urgent and emergency care is prioritised.
drivers: NHS England states resident-doctor industrial action runs from 07:00 on 7 April to 06:59 on 13 April (England)
NHS England warns the strike period is expected to be particularly challenging due to timing over Easter/bank holidays
Even with mitigations, strike action tends to drive cancellations/rescheduling and adds to existing service pressure

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: The advisory environment remains elevated, with the NCSC urging immediate action to mitigate newly disclosed vulnerabilities in widely deployed Citrix NetScaler products, consistent with ongoing exploitation pressure against internet-facing edge systems.
drivers: NCSC urges UK organisations to take immediate action to mitigate Citrix NetScaler vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-3055, CVE-2026-4368) affecting customer-managed deployments
NCSC recommends urgent upgrades to fixed NetScaler versions (14.1-66.59+, 13.1-62.23+, 13.1-FIPS/NDcPP 13.1.37.262+)
Persistent exploitation pressure for perimeter/edge appliances keeps baseline UK cyber risk elevated

indicators.name: Brent crude (Brent - Europe spot)
indicators.note: FRED series DCOILBRENTEU (EIA Brent Europe spot) shows most recent observation 127.61 USD/bbl on 2026-04-02 (page updated Apr 8, 2026). https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DCOILBRENTEU

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (y/y)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI inflation 3.0% in the 12 months to February 2026 (released 25 March 2026). https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/february2026

indicators.name: Bank Rate (UK)
indicators.note: Bank of England: MPC maintained Bank Rate at 3.75% (published 19 March 2026) and noted conflict-driven energy/commodity price rises will increase near-term CPI inflation. https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy-summary-and-minutes/2026/march-2026

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

indicators.name: Resident doctor industrial action (England)
indicators.note: NHS England: industrial action begins 7am on Tuesday 7 April and runs until 6:59am on Monday 13 April. https://www.england.nhs.uk/2026/04/nhs-urges-public-not-to-delay-seeking-medical-help-ahead-of-difficult-strike/

changes_since_last_update: Brent crude indicator source switched to FRED (EIA Brent Europe spot) due to Trading Economics page not being accessible without consent in this environment.
Social Stability drivers updated with NHS England’s published strike window and service-availability messaging.
priority_watch_items: Energy spillovers: monitor for renewed upward pressure on wholesale energy and fuel prices and associated UK inflation re-acceleration.
NHS resilience: monitor strike impacts, elective cancellations, and any escalation to critical incidents/OPEL pressures during the remainder of the action window.
Edge-vulnerability exposure: monitor for UK-targeted exploitation activity against internet-facing NetScaler systems and follow-on NCSC updates.
Geopolitical spillovers: monitor for any broadening of Middle East and Russia–Ukraine risks that could affect shipping routes, insurance, or supply.
Severe weather: monitor official flood and weather warnings for fast-changing local disruptions.
source_notes: This update prioritises official UK sources (BoE, ONS, MI5, NHS England, NCSC) plus a US Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED) series for Brent spot pricing derived from EIA data.
Some feeds are not real-time (e.g., the Brent spot series is published with a lag); intraday trading prices may differ.
Where statuses reflect advisory context (e.g., cyber vulnerabilities), they indicate elevated baseline risk rather than confirmed UK national-scale outages.
